text.title: php-laravel-patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for php-laravel-patterns. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Controllers must only: validate via FormRequest, call one service method, wrap result in Resource, return JsonResponse. No Eloquent queries, no business logic.
rule.rationale: Business logic in controllers cannot be unit-tested without booting HTTP; service isolation makes each concern independently testable and replaceable.

rule.statement: Services must not call request() or access any HTTP globals; accept primitives or DTOs from the controller.
rule.rationale: Framework-coupled services cannot be tested without booting Laravel; plain constructors with injected repositories keep services testable with Mockery alone.

rule.statement: Always use FormRequest::validated() when passing data to the service; never use request()->all() or $request->input().
rule.rationale: validated() returns only declared fields, eliminating mass-assignment risk without requiring $fillable checks inside the service.

rule.statement: Return JsonResource from controllers and plain Model/DTO from services; never return JsonResponse from a service.
rule.rationale: Leaking JsonResponse into the service layer couples it to HTTP; services should be callable from queued jobs, CLI commands, and tests without HTTP context.

rule.statement: Use DB::transaction(fn () => ...) not manual begin/commit/rollback; let exceptions propagate.
rule.rationale: Closure-based transactions auto-rollback on any exception; manual begin/commit requires remembering to rollback in every catch branch — easy to miss.

rule.statement: Do not wrap DB::transaction in try/catch that swallows the exception; let it bubble to the controller's exception handler.
rule.rationale: Swallowing exceptions inside a transaction closure defeats automatic rollback and hides bugs from the log. Laravel's exception handler maps exceptions to HTTP responses.

rule.statement: Add a repository only when: (a) multiple data sources exist, (b) you need to mock DB without a DB, or (c) query encapsulation is explicitly required. Default: inject the model directly.
rule.rationale: Eloquent is already an Active Record; a repository wrapping it adds ceremony without value unless there is a real abstraction need. LLMs over-prescribe repositories.

rule.statement: Bind RepositoryInterface to RepositoryImpl only if an alternative implementation exists or is planned; skip the interface for single-implementation repositories.
rule.rationale: Interface-first without a second implementation is premature abstraction; it adds two files and a service provider binding for zero benefit.
